hero.subtitle: We combine strategy, adoption, and automation, so AI works in 4 weeks and is yours in 6 months.

cases.sectionSubtitle: From insurance to real estate and marketing. Solutions that continue to work, even when we're gone.

movir.name: Movir
movir.quote: "SPAIK first trained our management team, then the entire company. Now everyone uses AI daily, with effective results, and the focus is on Marketing & Sales."
movir.author: Maurick Schellekens - CEO
movir.situation: Movir is a disability insurer with over 400 employees. The management team recognized AI's potential, but the organization was stuck in wait-and-see mode. There was no structure, no policy, and no visibility into what AI could actually deliver. The commercial department spent hours on manual work that could be done in minutes with the right approach.
movir.approach: We started with the management team. A session with Maurick and the MT examining what AI concretely means for Movir, where the opportunities lie, and what steps are needed. The MT was immediately convinced and wanted it for the entire organization. What followed: over 300 people trained in groups, using their own processes as the starting point. 10 internal champions trained to build independently. Then the marketing department in multiple sessions. Now we are working on the entire commercial side — sales and marketing — in two-week sprints.
movir.result: Sales conversations are automatically logged in the CRM, variables are filled, and detailed segmentation is created. What used to take an hour of sales processing is now one minute. Over 300 people trained, 10 Champions independently building AI applications, from technical solutions to agents. Movir grew from an initial training into an ongoing partnership.
movir.impact: Movir's commercial team delivers significantly more output with the same people. Sales spends time on clients instead of administration. Marketing creates in minutes what used to take hours. The 10 internal Champions ensure Movir isn't dependent on external parties: they identify new use cases and build on their own. The management team showed remarkable openness from the start: everyone deserves to be well-prepared for this.

euphoria.name: Euphoria Mobility
euphoria.quote: "Our developers built in hours what would normally take weeks. Even the skeptics became enthusiastic. No theory, just building. Two days later: 6 working prototypes."
euphoria.author: Vincent Bekker - CTO
euphoria.situation: Euphoria Mobility is a taxi and mobility company with 60+ employees, a fleet of 500 vehicles, and 20+ years of operational data. The company wanted to structurally adopt AI but the organization had mixed readiness — from beginners to skeptics. The development team knew AI could accelerate their work but didn't know where to start. CTO Vincent Bekker didn't want months of strategy — he wanted proof.
euphoria.approach: We took a phased approach. First, two Inspiration sessions for the entire company — not just developers, everyone. Then a Fundamentals deep dive with a core group. When the team was ready: a two-day AI hackathon with 25 developers in 6 teams. No slides, building directly on real Euphoria data (100,000+ rides, 500 vehicles, 1,000+ drivers). Each team got a concrete business challenge with SPAIK guidance. Recently, we started an AMA Bot PoC — an on-premise AI assistant that makes 240,000+ Zendesk tickets and thousands of Wiki pages searchable for the operations team.
euphoria.result: The hackathon produced 6 working prototypes in 2 days, 3 of which went directly to production. Participants rated the hackathon 4.2/5, knowledge increase scored 4.5/5. Skeptics were converted: "Build a PoC in very little time and with some more work, something that could go to production." The AMA Bot PoC has been successfully completed — from hackathon idea to working product. Claude Code was by far the most valued tool among developers.
euphoria.impact: Euphoria grew from "nobody knows where to start with AI" to an organization that structurally uses AI. The 3 applications from the hackathon run in production. The AMA Bot makes 15 years of scattered knowledge accessible to a young operations team. The partnership continues to evolve — a kind of adoption light — from training to hackathon to concrete products.

reditus.name: Reditus
reditus.quote: "What set SPAIK apart was their pragmatic approach and seniority. Experienced individuals who add value from day one."
reditus.author: Joran Hofman - CEO
reditus.roiText: From idea to live product in 4 months
reditus.situation: Reditus, an affiliate marketing platform for B2B SaaS companies, had their biggest growth opportunity clearly in sight: automatically finding relevant affiliates outside their 20,000+ network. But the engineering team had no bandwidth for R&D. Manual affiliate recruitment took half a day per client, that doesn't scale to hundreds of clients. Competitors were launching similar features. Reditus had SaaStock in October as a deadline to demo the feature live.
reditus.approach: SPAIK worked as a forward-deployed engineer: integrated into the Reditus team, daily standups, direct system access. Week 1-2: a deliberately simple PoC with Google Docs and Sheets to validate whether AI agents could produce workable results. It worked. Then production architecture: n8n workflows, Supabase as data layer, three parallel AI agents (Content Affiliate, Service Provider, On-Page Crawl). Each agent follows a 3-layer process: collection, analysis, enrichment. The transfer model: SPAIK builds an agent to stable MVP, documents everything, transfers to Reditus, and starts on the next.
reditus.result: What used to take 4-6 hours of manual research per client is now 30 minutes of automated processing with 1,000-2,000+ qualified matches. 17,000+ new affiliate prospects discovered, 26,000+ contacts verified. 6 paying beta clients specifically for the AI feature. Live demo at SaaStock generated 20+ qualified leads. Full code transfer to the Reditus team. The retainer ended because the project succeeded, not because it failed.
reditus.impact: Reditus now has a fully integrated AI module in their platform that transformed their affiliate discovery from manual to hyperscale. The competitive difference: they did it in 4 months where comparable features take 12-18 months. The codebase is transferable and maintainable, no vendor lock-in. The Reditus team runs, debugs, and extends the system independently. SPAIK didn't build for Reditus, but with Reditus.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How long does a full implementation take?
faq.a1: The Kickstart lasts 4 weeks. Fully independent teams typically take 6 months on average.
faq.q2: How do you ensure it continues to work after departure?
faq.a2: We train key figures in your team, people who take ownership. They not only learn how it works but also how to manage it and build new automations themselves.
faq.q3: What if our data or systems are not yet ready for AI?
faq.a3: That's why we start with a Tech assessment. We analyze your data and systems and prepare them for AI as we build.
faq.q4: Does this work with our current legacy systems?
faq.a4: We maintain the old system intact, but build a smart, flexible layer on top. This approach always works.
faq.a5: We always start with a proof of concept in the Kickstart. If it doesn't work, you know within 4 weeks, before you've invested heavily.
faq.a6: That's exactly what we recommend. Start with one process, prove the value, then scale.
faq.a7: Your team manages it independently. We remain available for questions, but you no longer need us for daily operations.
faq.q8: Do we need internal tech people to get started?
faq.a8: Not necessarily. We bring the technical expertise. What you need are people who know the processes and are motivated to improve them.
faq.q9: Do your training courses comply with the EU AI Act?
faq.a9: Yes. Our Fundamentals training covers AI literacy as required by the EU AI Act, including responsible use and risk classification.
faq.q10: Are you replacing people with AI?
faq.a10: No. We automate tasks, not jobs. The goal is to free up your people to do more valuable work.

cta.title: Discover if we're a match?

adoptie.metaDescription: From proof to ownership. 6 months of dedicated collaboration until your team can do it themselves.
adoptie.title: Adoption
adoptie.headline: From proof to ownership
adoptie.pitch: A program with a clear beginning and end. 6 months of intensive collaboration, building, transferring, and training Champions, until you can do it yourselves. Continuing with us afterwards is a choice, not a necessity.
adoptie.marketInsightTitle: Not because of technology, because of adoption
adoptie.marketInsightBody: Most AI projects don't fail because the technology doesn't work, but because nobody takes ownership. No champions, no process for embedding it. After the pilot, everyone goes back to the old way of working.
adoptie.alt1Problem: Takes months longer, knowledge stays with 1-2 people, and it fades at the first deadline.
adoptie.alt1SpaikDiff: We accelerate and embed simultaneously. After 6 months it stands, even when we leave.
adoptie.alt2Problem: Inspiring, but after 2 weeks nobody does anything with it. Knowledge without implementation evaporates.
adoptie.alt2SpaikDiff: Training is part of our program, not the whole program. We build and train.
adoptie.alt3Problem: Months of analysis, PowerPoint deliverables, and a shocking invoice. Your team learns nothing.
adoptie.alt3SpaikDiff: We work alongside your team, not in front of it. Everything we build, you can maintain.
adoptie.alt4Problem: 6 months recruiting, 6 months onboarding, single point of failure. And one person can't do everything.
adoptie.alt4SpaikDiff: We deliver a senior team immediately, while training your own Champions.
adoptie.tandemSubtitle: We start in front, but the goal is for you to ride on your own.
adoptie.tandemPhase1Body: We steer, build the first automations, and identify your Champions.
adoptie.tandemPhase2Body: Champions take over more and more. We guide, you do increasingly more yourself.
adoptie.tandemPhase3Body: Champions are self-sufficient. SPAIK available for questions, no longer leading.
adoptie.championsSubtitle: Not dependency, but ownership. Your Champions learn to identify, build, embed, and sell internally.
adoptie.champ1Body: How do you bring people along, and how do you free up budget internally for AI initiatives?
adoptie.champ2Body: How do you find the right problems to solve? Process analysis, impact estimation, prioritization.
adoptie.champ3Body: Prompts, workflows, tools, testing, documentation, from idea to production and maintenance.
adoptie.champ4Body: Privacy, risk assessment, EU AI Act, escalation protocol, filled in sector-specifically.
adoptie.certBody: Practical test + portfolio review. SPAIK Certified AI Champion certificate and LinkedIn badge.
adoptie.kitBody: ROI templates, internal pitch deck, management FAQ, so Champions can also free up budget internally.
adoptie.communityBody: Closed community for certified Champions. Knowledge sharing, best practices, quarterly meetups.

whySpaik.title: Why SPAIK?
whySpaik.subtitle: Most companies try it themselves or hire a traditional consultancy. Both stall on the same thing: adoption.

row3.spaik: Strategy + adoption + build in one team

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: Will we become dependent on you?
faq.a1: The exact opposite. Our entire model is built on transferring ownership. After the Kickstart you have a roadmap and working automation. After Adoption you manage and build yourself. Continuing with us is a choice, not a necessity.
faq.a2: With the Kickstart you pay €5,000 upfront. After week 1 there's a checkpoint. Doesn't fit? Stop, pay only that €5k. Handshake, done.
faq.q3: We already use Copilot / ChatGPT, why SPAIK?
faq.a3: 80% of AI licenses go unused after the first month. We make sure it lands in your processes and people actually use it. The tool isn't the problem, adoption is.
faq.q4: How long until we see results?
faq.a4: 4 weeks. In a Kickstart the first automation is live, your team is trained, and you have a roadmap for the rest. No months-long projects.
faq.q5: What makes SPAIK different from other AI agencies?
faq.a5: The combination. We're not the best at strategy, not the best at training, not the best at automation, but the combination of all three in one team is rare. That's why adoption actually sticks with us.

brochure.documentSubtitle: From proof to ownership in 6 months
brochure.introBody: 70% of AI pilots fail, not because of technology, but because of poor adoption. Meanwhile, organizations that do follow through gain a 12-18 month head start. The question isn't whether you'll use AI, but how quickly your team can own it.
brochure.introBody2: A dedicated team that works alongside your people for 6 months, trains Champions, and transfers everything. Afterwards, AI belongs to you, not us.
brochure.tandemBody: SPAIK starts in front, but the goal is for you to ride on your own. Our involvement decreases from 80% to 5%, your ownership grows.
brochure.tierNote: All programs are 6 months. The difference is in depth and scope of transformation.
brochure.outcomesTitle: What it delivers to your organization
brochure.outcome1: Certified AI Champions who drive and embed it internally
brochure.outcome2: Working automations that immediately save time and money
brochure.outcome3: A governance framework for safe and responsible AI use
brochure.outcome4: Full knowledge transfer, documentation, training materials, management guide
brochure.outcome5: Independence, continuing with us is a choice, not a necessity
brochure.governanceBody: Standard in every program: when AI is/isn't appropriate, data ownership, GDPR compliance, sector-specific rules. Not a premium add-on.
brochure.riskTitle: Three guarantees that protect your investment
brochure.risk1Body: We don't sell a program if there are no suitable Champions. No good match = no sale.
brochure.risk2Body: Most clients start with a Kickstart (€12,500). After week 1, there's a go/no-go moment.
brochure.risk3Body: After 6 months, everything is yours, documentation, training materials, working systems. No lock-in.
brochure.aboutBody: SPAIK combines strategy, adoption, and automation, so AI works in 4 weeks and belongs to you after 6 months. 35+ implementations, 9.1/10 evaluations, from insurance to real estate and marketing.
brochure.testimonialQuote: I've worked with multiple parties, but I see SPAIK as a true partner that thinks along with us and proactively acts.
brochure.ctaBody: Schedule a no-obligation 30-minute conversation. We'll discuss your situation and whether the Adoption program is the right step.

notList.title: What we don't do
notList.subtitle: How we work is partly defined by what we refuse.
notList.item1Body: We don't deliver strategy decks that end up in a drawer. Everything we advise, we also build. First automation live within 4 weeks.
notList.item2Body: A license isn't a solution. The value of AI is in its use, not its purchase. We make sure your team actually uses it, measurably.
notList.item3Title: No juniors learning on your dime
notList.item3Body: A founder or senior lead is always on your project. We train our people internally, not at your expense.
notList.item4Body: At the end of every phase, we hand over everything so you can continue without us. Working with us is a choice, not a necessity.
notList.item5Body: We only build with AI when it demonstrably adds value. Sometimes a simple automation without AI is better, then we choose that.
